PulseDex_2026-06-25_equiv.node-export.js.note: ADDED 2026-06-25 (SIGNAL-ADAPTER-FOLLOWUPS-VI §1) — PulseDex's FIRST code-gated fixture + the input for the Phase-9 'compute() ≡ committed export' equivalence gate (env.equiv.pulsedex). Produced by running the current PulseDex DSP (the code bundled into PulseDex.html, manifestHash 3c85d78cd9c2) headless on the committed Polar H10 RR input: PulseDex.parseRRInput(text) → PulseDex.compute({intervals,tsMs,t0Ms,offsetMin}) → this ganglior.node-export (534 usable beats, spot reading 7.3 min, 0 windowed events at current thresholds). This IS the app's exportGanglior shape — pulsedex-app.js delegates to the SAME pdBuildNodeExport, so the fixture is byte-identical to PulseDex.html's own export (the headless compute() leaves schema.generated/provenance/kernel as volatile metadata, which the gate's EXCL strips). Fully regenerate-able: re-run PulseDex.parseRRInput + .compute on the committed RR txt; the equivalence-gate diff (excludes generated/provenance/kernel/vo2est/karv) reproduces it with 0 differing fields. GATE B asserts the producing bundle's CURRENT manifestHash still equals 3c85d78cd9c2 — turns red the moment PulseDex code changes. NO app re-bundle (the fixture is produced BY the current gate-clean bundle).

PulseDex_2026-06-25_events.node-export.j.note: ADDED 2026-06-25 (SIGNAL-ADAPTER-FOLLOWUPS-VII §2) — event-byte-coverage for the equivalence gate (env.equiv.pulsedex_events). The -VI PulseDex equiv fixture (a 7.3-min spot reading) emitted ZERO events, and committed real overnight Polar RR files are from a healthy subject (a 7.25h file tested → 0 events), so neither real input exercises the windowed event branch. uploads/PulseDex_2026-06-25_events_RR.txt is a deterministic synthetic Polar-format overnight RR (92 min, 6153 beats, ISO-stamped) with one low-HRV 5-min window (rMSSD≈10, SDNN≈5) that crosses BOTH the windowed thresholds: rmssd<0.7*median && <25 → hrv_drop, and stressEst>=70 → stress_peak. Produced by parseRRInput → PulseDex.compute({intervals,tsMs,t0Ms,offsetMin}) on the current PulseDex bundle (manifestHash 3c85d78cd9c2 — NO PulseDex re-bundle this round) → 2 events (hrv_drop + stress_peak at 22:30). This IS pdBuildNodeExport's output (the app delegates to it). Fully regenerate-able: re-run the same seam on the committed RR txt; the gate diff (excludes generated/provenance/kernel) reproduces it with 0 differing fields. GATE B asserts the producing bundle's CURRENT manifestHash still equals 3c85d78cd9c2.

synthetic_pulsedex_golden.node-export.js.note: ADDED 2026-07-12 (CPAP-REAL-CORPUS-2026-07-11-BRIEF §P9, resolving §M5) — a COMMITTED, content-addressed equivalence input for PulseDex. Before P9, every node's equivalence input was a REAL recording, hence gitignored, hence absent on a fresh clone: ALL EIGHT compute()-equivalence legs SKIPPED in CI. The suite's strongest correctness gate — the GATE-C surface that catches a code change which moved a fixture's OUTPUT — therefore ran only on the maintainer's machine, and a regression that moved an export would have gone green through CI. This input is SYNTHETIC (tools/make-synthetic-inputs.mjs): the vendor FORMAT is reproduced exactly, the DATA never is — closed-form, physiologically plausible waveforms containing no recording of any person and no device identifier. Deterministic (no RNG, no Date.now()), so re-running the generator reproduces it byte-for-byte and inputHashes stay stable. It ADDS to the real-recording leg (which still runs locally and exercises genuine vendor quirks); it does not replace it. REGENERATE the input and this golden together whenever the export shape changes.
